items.title: JHEEM modeling platform
items.description: A public HIV-modeling platform spanning the interactive product, model-execution workflows, versioned simulation data, and cloud delivery. I designed the system and implemented its frontend, orchestration, and integration layers.
evidence: One model configuration drives workflows and frontend behavior
On-demand model runs with cached, shareable results
Reusable orchestration cut workflow code by 54%

items.title: Reproducible model execution & delivery
items.description: The execution layer behind JHEEM: reproducible R model images, versioned simulation artifacts, numerical regression checks, and production-safe release workflows. I am currently consolidating five container repositories into a history-preserving monorepo with path-aware matrix CI.
evidence: Four model images reproduce production outputs bit-for-bit
Digest-pinned base image and renv-locked dependencies
Monorepo migration preserves image names and production pins

items.title: Digital twin of the infant microbiome
items.description: First-author research developing a generative, patient-specific model of infant microbiome dynamics to forecast ecosystem trajectories and identify early signals associated with neurodevelopmental risk.
evidence: Forecasted microbial abundance dynamics with R² = 0.69

items.title: Tech2Check intervention modeling
items.description: An active HIV intervention-modeling study in JHEEM. I designed the intervention lifecycle, built the calibrated analysis and verification pipeline, and used decomposition and sensitivity analysis to distinguish structural limits from implementation assumptions.

items.title: Computational Epidemiology Lab site + Global HIV Aging app
items.description: The public web presence for a Johns Hopkins modeling group, including an interactive application for exploring country-level HIV aging projections and the evidence behind them.
evidence: Research findings and publications presented for mixed audiences
